rulings.Case Title: LYNNE CRAWFORD VS. ROBERT J. LIS, AN INDIVIDUAL DOING BUSINESS AS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Against Defendant Timothy Lis Dba As Lis Construction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 9, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT TIMOTHY LIS DBA LIS CONSTRUCTION's Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Against Defendant Timothy Lis Dba As Lis Construction Defendant Timothy Lis' motion to set aside default and default judgment is granted in part. The default entered against Timothy Lis on June 25, 2018 is vacated on the condition that must be accepted by Timothy Lis that he will not assert any argument that any claims of plaintiff Lynne Crawford against him are barred by the five year statute. No default judgment has been entered against Timothy Lis, so that portion of the motion is off calendar. Timothy Lis' motion papers did not request vacating a default entered against him prior to June 25, 2018 nor did those papers discuss the effect of the granting of this motion or the subsequent filing of an amended complaint or a request for default on the continued vitality of a default entered against him prior to June 25, 2018 and thus the court does not address the effect, if any, of a default entered against Timothy Lis prior to June 25, 2018. Timothy Lis has shown good cause to set aside the June 25, 2018 based on surprise, inadvertence, mistake and excusable neglect per CCP 473(b) due to his mistaken belief that his brother and general contractor on the project would handle his defense for him without need for him to take action. Vacating the June 25, 2018 default also comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits, especially where, as here, plaintiff Lynne Crawford has not identified any legal prejudice to her if the motion is granted other than the possible application of the five year statute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Timothy Lis is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must include language that Timothy Lis consents to the condition not to assert any argument based on CCP 583.310 and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN RYAN VS. 1001 CALIFORNIA STREET CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Martin Ryan And Claire Ryan'S Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 9, 2019, Line 1, Plaintiffs Martin Ryan And Claire Ryan's Motion To Compel. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants shall promptly provide to the Court for in camera review an unredacted copy of Mr. Hughes November 11, 2013 letter to the HOA. The Court shall attempt to determine whether such letter contains Mr. Hughes' thoughts, impressions or legal analysis and will issue an appropriate order thereafter. No sanctions. Both parties were substantially justified in their arguments. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WENDY HOUGH ET AL VS. TODD COURAGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs Requessts For Documents, Set One Number 1-45; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Christopher Scorfina And His Attorney Of Record, Todd Angstadt, In The Amount Of $1,717
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 9, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFFS WENDY HOUGH, MATTHEW HRUDKA'S Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs Requessts For Documents, Set One Number 1-45; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Christopher Scorfina And His Attorney Of Record, Todd Angstadt, In The Amount Of $1,717. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted in part and denied in part. CCP section 2031.310(g) provides that the court shall limit the extent of discovery of ESI if the information sought can be obtained more conveniently from another source or if the discovery sought is unreasonably cumulative or duplicative. This court cannot find any equivalent Code section that applies specifically to documents that are not stored electronically, but believes CCP section 2031.310(g) is applicable here by analogy. Here, Mr. Scorfina's responses, Bates stamped DEF 01- DEF0271, are alleged to be identical to his co-defendant's, Mr. Courage. However, his written responses and verification do not specifically state that his responses are identical to Mr. Courage's. Plaintiff is entitled to a verified response which states this fact, if true. To the extent, Mr. Scorfina's documents are not identical in all respects to Mr. Courage's, then he is obligated to produce such non-identical documents. For example, if Mr. Scorfina has similar documents with margin notes or other differences, then such documents are not identical and the request is not cumulative. Mr. Scorfina shall, within 10 days, serve a verification stating, if true, that his only responsive documents (DEF 01- DEF0271) are identical in all respects to those produced by Mr. Courage. Within that time period, he must also serve responsive non identical documents, if any. It is not entirely clear from the pleadings whether other responses are at issue. No sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL S. KECK VS. ERIC M. SAFIRE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 9, 2019, Line 7, DEFENDANT ERIC SAFIRE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Defendant Eric Safire's demurrer to both causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff Michael Keck is sustained without leave to amend. The face of the complaint shows that the statute of limitations on both of Mr. Keck's claims began to run no later than June 5, 2015 when Mr. Keck filed what he refers to as the quantum meruit action. The two year statute of limitations in CCP 335.1 and/or CCP 339(1) applies to both of Mr. Keck's claims. Since this case was not filed till 2018, both of Mr. Keck's claims are time-barred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Mr. Safire is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: MINSOO AN INDIVIDUAL KIM VS. WONHONG "WILL" LEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Dismiss Or, Alternatively, Stay The Action, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 9, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT INSTQUEST INC. DBA VERLOCAL's Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Dismiss Or, Alternatively, Stay The Action Defendant Instquest Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration is granted. This case is stayed pending the conclusion of arbitration proceedings. The FAA applies to this case because it involves interstate commerce as acknowledged in the complaint. Instquest is a Delaware corporation and its on-line services implicate interstate commerce. Paragraph 13 of the parties' agreement expressly states that the FAA will govern with the limited exception that the procedural rules of the CAA apply. The FAA preempts application of Labor Code 229, which allows an employee to litigate wage claims notwithstanding an arbitration agreement. (Hoover v. American Income Life Ins. Co. (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 1193, 1207.) The agreement has an enforceable delegation clause, so the arbitrator will decide the scope and proper parties to the arbitration proceeding. Paragraph 13B provides that the JAMS employment rules apply. JAMS employment Rule 11(b), which was promulgated on July 1, 2014, provides that the arbitrator decides all jurisdictional and arbitrability disputes. The parties entered into their agreement on July 27, 2014 and they clearly and unmistakably delegated the issue of arbitrability to the arbitrator by referencing these rules in their agreement. (Dream Theater, Inc. v. Dream Theater (2004) 124 Cal.App.4th 547, 557.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Instquest is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)
